description: LoRA fine-tuning (r=16, merged) to 100% canonical-48 accuracy with the canonical-48 held out of training; 3 seeds at 7B, 1 at 1.5B; measure how the invariance bands change pre vs post.
motivation: Test the 'fine-tuning installs a rule' claim by watching whether FT adds a rule signature or just deepens existing bands.
formal_results: 600 LoRA steps, 21/48 -> 48/48 (BODMAS-LTR gap ~1 -> ~13), seed-stable. Mid band L20 deepens d=-2.20 -> {-4.78,-3.91,-5.02} (~2.2x, 3 seeds); 1.5B L16 -0.61 -> -3.86 (6x); late band extends from 1 layer (L23) to 3 (L23-L25). Exp-G at L22 post-FT: rejected (supports lookup).
results_description: Fine-tuning amplifies both load-bearing bands 2-6x seed-stably and extends the late band, but Exp-G rejects a staged b*c rule post-FT.
conclusions: Fine-tuning AMPLIFIES the pretrained layered profile rather than installing a general rule - it augments alongside an existing lookup.
